rationale: Iran deal progress is the most relevant event for Portugal — potential energy price relief; NATO asset cuts have only indirect EU defense debate relevance for this western-periphery lens.

top_events.title: US-Iran deal reported closer than ever
top_events.summary: A US-Iran nuclear/sanctions deal could meaningfully lower global oil and gas prices, offering indirect cost-of-living relief to Portuguese households and supporting the tourism-dependent economy.

top_events.title: US to cut air and naval assets for NATO Europe
top_events.summary: US NATO asset reductions add pressure to EU defense autonomy debates; limited direct security impact on Portugal but relevant to EU-wide defense spending and budget priorities.

top_events.title: UN adopts treaty setting standards for gig economy workers
top_events.summary: A new UN treaty on gig economy labor standards could influence EU and Portuguese labor policy, with mild positive implications for platform workers in Portugal's growing gig sector.
